timeline.label: Q1 2026 results: $102.9M adjusted EBITDA on ~2.5M bbl/d; 2026 EBITDA guide $425M–$465M and capex $430M–$490M.
FY2025 Form 10-K: $254.0M adjusted EBITDA, $525.6M revenue, ~$1.46B debt; ~10.4-year weighted contract life.
Valerie Chase appointed independent director (term through 2026 annual meeting).
IPO and combination with WaterBridge Resources LLC; Class A listing; sponsor Five Point retains significant control.
Insider purchases by Crane family and Gregory Daily at ~$20/share at IPO pricing.
Sponsor and Devon Energy sold ~17.5M+ shares in secondary offering near $30; large insider selling context.
Gravity Water acquisition referenced as comparable; smaller basin transaction near ~5.5× run-rate EBITDA.
Kraken and combination projects expanded Delaware network scale ahead of IPO.

industry.structure: Delaware Basin produced-water midstream is concentrated among a few scaled operators with pipeline density, disposal capacity, and long-term E&P dedications; merchant pricing, not regulated rate base.
industry.share_shift: WaterBridge gained share through IPO combination scale and brownfield expansion; Gravity and smaller private operators remain fragmented competitors.
industry.trend: Higher water intensity per well and basin consolidation support infrastructure demand; 2026 is a heavy growth-capex year (Speedway, Devon-supporting projects) with leverage and sponsor overhang as offsets.
